predictedBugs: COD-1: sanitize_url_param() applied to existing param values that already contain URL-encoded sequences could double-encode, causing API rejection
COD-2: Changing User-Agent from 'SmartLic/1.0' to 'Extra-Consultoria/1.0' in sync/async PNCP clients could trigger API access denial if the API validates by UA
COD-3: make_url() in security.py may break crawlers that expect None params to be sent as empty string instead of omitted
edgeCases: EDGE-1: Param values with unicode characters (e.g., 'licitação 2024') - sanitize_url_param handles via %XX encoding
EDGE-2: Param values containing '&' or '=' characters which could break URL structure - sanitize_url_param encodes these
EDGE-3: Empty param values should still be sent as empty string, not omitted
